entries.title: Conditional routing added
entries.problem: V1 only supported linear journeys. Real services have conditional routing — different paths based on answers. Designers needed prototypes that reflect this without writing routing logic themselves.
entries.approach: Updated the prompt schema so radio options carry a next field pointing to any page in the journey. Updated the generator to emit if/else routing logic from that schema rather than linear redirects. Increased the token budget to 8000 to support more complex journeys.
whatChanged: Radio options now use structured objects with text, value, and next fields
Generator emits conditional redirects based on next field values
Ineligibility can be triggered by any answer on any question
Separate v2 prompt and generator files — v1 untouched
entries.nextSteps: Stability testing on complex briefs. Once confirmed stable, v3 will add task lists, interruption cards, content pages, and date inputs.

entries.title: PDF handling improved
entries.problem: PDF uploads made no visible difference to generated prototypes. Raw PDF text was truncated aggressively, not summarised intelligently, and Claude had no instruction on how to use it. Large documents would produce worse results than small ones.
entries.approach: Added a dedicated PDF summarisation step before prototype generation. Claude reads the brief and the document together and extracts only the relevant content — eligibility rules, evidence requirements, process steps, timeframes, and key terminology. Documents over roughly 100 pages are truncated before summarisation and the user is informed. If the summarisation call fails, generation continues without it.
whatChanged: New PDF summarisation Claude call before main generation call
Summarisation is brief-aware — extracts only content relevant to the service
Documents over 15000 characters truncated to 80000 characters before summarisation
User shown a notice if their document was too long to read in full
New progress step: Reading document — visible only when a PDF is attached
PDF improvements applied to both v1 and v2
entries.nextSteps: Test with real policy PDFs of varying lengths to confirm summary quality and content improvement in generated prototypes.

entries.title: Structured intake form
entries.problem: A single free-text brief produced generic output because Claude had too little context to make good content decisions.
entries.approach: Replaced the single textarea with three structured fields that mirror how a content designer thinks: users and needs, content and language, blockers and resolutions. The three fields are combined into a structured brief before being sent to Claude.
whatChanged: Single brief textarea replaced with three structured fields on v1 and v2
Fields: users and needs, content and language, blockers and resolutions
Server combines fields into labelled brief before Claude call
Validation updated to require the first field at minimum
entries.nextSteps: Test across a range of briefs to confirm richer input produces better prototype content.
